iterations.strategy: Root cause: the iter_0 plan uses 64 thread-local direct arrays with 65536 aggregate slots each, so the runtime allocates and zero-initializes about 256 MiB of aggregation state to produce only about 6 groups; that cache-exceeding, mostly unused state dominates total time even though the actual scan is already fast. Strategy category B/C: keep the zone-map pruning plan, but replace the oversized direct array with a tiny cache-resident thread-local hash table sized to the actual group count, split accepted blocks from boundary blocks so accepted blocks never touch row-level shipdate values, and merge only touched groups before the final sort.

Root cause: the current plan assumes shipdate zone-map pruning will help, but all 600 lineitem shipdate blocks are boundary blocks, so the runtime still performs a full 59.99M-row scan while paying extra block classification, atomic block scheduling, and per-row compact-hash probes to produce only 4 groups. Strategy category A/D: remove the ineffective zone-map-driven accept/boundary path from the hot loop, scan contiguous row ranges directly with the l_shipdate <= 10471 predicate inline, map the four observed (l_returnflag,l_linestatus) combinations to dense ids with a tiny lookup table, aggregate into a 4-slot thread-local direct array that fits in L1, then merge 4 slots per thread and sort the 4 output rows.

Root cause: the scan/aggregation kernel is already close to the machine's bandwidth limit, reading about 2.28 GB of fixed-width lineitem data in 33.8 ms while the aggregation state is only about 20 KB across all threads, so the remaining gap to 75 ms is unnecessary post-query teardown work from destroying seven large mmaps inside the measured total; additionally, the hot loop still pays avoidable 128-bit arithmetic on every qualifying row even though Q1's scale-2 value bounds keep sum_disc_price and sum_charge within signed 64-bit range. Strategy category C: keep the full-table sequential scan with the inline shipdate predicate and 4-slot direct aggregation, but retain mmaps until process exit / after the total timer ends so munmap page-table cleanup is off the hot path, and use 64-bit accumulation throughout the scan to minimize per-row arithmetic cost while preserving the validated scaling formulas.
